openmeter/billing/creditgrant.purpose: Credit-grant-centric facade over the charges layer exposing Create, Get, List, and UpdateExternalSettlement for creditpurchase.Charge values. Holds no Ent client — all persistence flows through the injected charges.Service; owns input validation and customer-ownership checks only.

patterns.name: Config+Validate/New constructor
patterns.description: New(c Config) calls c.Validate() before returning the service; dependencies enter only through Config.
patterns.example: func New(c Config) (Service, error) { if err := c.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &service{chargesService: c.ChargesService}, nil }

patterns.name: input.Validate() at top of every method
patterns.description: Each public method calls input.Validate() before any business logic; validation wraps errors in models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)).
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return creditpurchase.Charge{}, err }

patterns.name: Delegate all persistence to charges.Service
patterns.description: No openmeter/ent/db imports; all reads/writes go through chargesService / creditpurchase pipeline.
patterns.example: charge, err := s.chargesService.GetByID(ctx, charges.GetByIDInput{ID: input.ChargeID})

patterns.name: Customer ownership check in service layer
patterns.description: Get-style methods assert charge.Intent.CustomerID == input.CustomerID after fetching; not enforced downstream.
patterns.example: if charge.Intent.CustomerID != input.CustomerID { return creditpurchase.Charge{}, models.NewGenericForbiddenError(...) }

patterns.name: FundingMethod enum with Validate()
patterns.description: FundingMethod.Validate() is invoked inside CreateInput.Validate(); funded grants (non-none) require non-nil Purchase terms.
patterns.example: if i.FundingMethod != FundingMethodNone && i.Purchase == nil { errs = append(errs, errors.New("purchase terms are required")) }

patterns.name: NoopService compile-time guard
patterns.description: noop.go provides NoopService{} with var _ Service = NoopService{} for credits-disabled wiring.
patterns.example: var _ Service = NoopService{}

key_file_guides.role: Service interface, all Input types with Validate(), FundingMethod enum, PurchaseTerms, GrantFilters, and the concrete service struct + New constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() must return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)); FundingMethod.Validate() must run inside CreateInput.Validate().

key_file_guides.role: No-op implementation returning zero-values for all methods when credits are disabled.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with the Service interface — add a method here whenever Service grows.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db or calling Ent builders directly — all DB access must flow through charges.Service or creditpurchase.Service
Skipping input.Validate() at the top of a new method
Returning a charge without verifying charge.Intent.CustomerID == input.CustomerID in Get-style methods
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx
Adding business logic inside toIntent()/toSettlement() converters beyond pure field mapping

decisions.decision: Service delegates entirely to charges.Service rather than holding its own adapter
decisions.rationale: Keeps a single authoritative write path for credit-purchase charges; avoids parallel DB access that could bypass transaction discipline.

decisions.decision: Ownership check done in the service layer, not in charges.Service
decisions.rationale: charges.Service is a generic multi-customer service; customer scoping is a caller responsibility enforced here.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a service method with input validation, ownership check, and delegation

openmeter/billing/models.purpose: Organisational container for shared billing domain model sub-packages — each pairs a Go domain struct with its co-located Ent mixin so schema and type stay in sync. No source files live directly here; children are creditsapplied, externalid, stddetailedline, and totals.

patterns.name: Ent mixin co-located with domain model
patterns.description: Each sub-package defines the domain struct in model.go and the matching Ent mixin in mixin.go in the same package.
patterns.example:
// model.go: type Totals struct {...}
// mixin.go: func (Mixin) Fields() []ent.Field {...}

patterns.name: Typed Setter/Getter generics over raw Ent mutations
patterns.description: externalid and totals expose generic Creator[T]/Setter[T]/Getter so adapters never call individual Set* Ent methods.
patterns.example: type Setter[T any] interface { SetTotals(T) *T }

patterns.name: Currency-precision rounding before persistence
patterns.description: Totals.RoundToPrecision(currency) must run before any DB write to prevent numeric drift; same discipline for creditsapplied sums.
patterns.example: t = t.RoundToPrecision(currencyx.Calculator{Currency: currency})

patterns.name: models.Validator on enums and structs
patterns.description: Enum and struct types implement Validate() error; enums enforce a positive/non-negative invariant (creditsapplied positivity, totals non-negative).
patterns.example: func (s Status) Validate() error { switch s { case ...: return nil; default: return fmt.Errorf("invalid status: %s", s) } }

patterns.name: goderive for equality on stddetailedline.Base
patterns.description: derived.gen.go is generated from generate.go annotations; never hand-edit the generated file.
patterns.example: //go:generate go run github.com/awalterschulze/goderive .

key_file_guides.role: Totals struct with Add, Sum, RoundToPrecision, Validate; non-negative invariant.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a field requires updating Add, Sub, Neg, Equal, IsZero, RoundToPrecision, Validate, mixin Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], and FromDB together.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin for invoicing/payment external-ID columns plus generic Creator/Updater/Getter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use lo.EmptyableToPtr for nullable string columns — never store empty strings; update LineExternalIDs.Equal when adding a field.

key_file_guides.role: Base struct for flat-fee and usage-based detailed lines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: child_unique_reference_id has a DB CHECK and is the line upsert identity — never make it optional; update Creator[T] and DBGetter on field changes.

key_file_guides.role: CreditsApplied slice with Validate (positivity) and currency-aware Sum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Accumulate via Sum with RoundToPrecision; use .Equal() not == on alpacadecimal.Decimal.
Regenerate via go generate after any Base field change — never hand-edit.

anti_patterns: Adding DB/Ent logic to model.go or domain logic to mixin.go — keep layers strictly separated
Hand-editing derived.gen.go instead of regenerating via go generate
Skipping RoundToPrecision before persisting or comparing Totals / CreditsApplied
Storing empty strings in nullable external-ID columns instead of using lo.EmptyableToPtr
Adding a new field to Totals/Base without updating every companion method and the mixin

decisions.decision: Mixin and domain model co-located in the same sub-package rather than a shared mixin layer
decisions.rationale: Keeps schema and Go representation in sync; a single file change updates both the DB column and its type.

decisions.decision: goderive for equality rather than hand-written Equal on stddetailedline.Base
decisions.rationale: Base has deeply nested pointer/slice fields; generated equality is complete and removes maintenance burden.

openmeter/billing/service.purpose: Concrete implementation of billing.Service — the central orchestrator for profiles, customer overrides, gathering and standard invoices, invoice lines, sequences, app resolution, lock acquisition, and the stateless-backed invoice state machine. All DB access is delegated to billing.Adapter via entutils transactions.

patterns.name: transactionForInvoiceManipulation wraps customer-mutating ops
patterns.description: Methods creating/updating invoices or lines call transactionForInvoiceManipulation, which UpsertCustomerLock (outside tx) then starts a tx and calls LockCustomerForUpdate inside it.
patterns.example: return transactionForInvoiceManipulation(ctx, s, input.Customer, func(ctx context.Context) (T, error) { ... })

patterns.name: transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue for adapter calls
patterns.description: Service methods wrap adapter access so Ent's ctx-bound transaction is propagated; never call the adapter bare from an exported method.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (billing.X, error) { return s.adapter.GetX(ctx, ...) })

patterns.name: InvoiceStateMachine pooled in sync.Pool
patterns.description: stdinvoicestate.go allocates from invoiceStateMachineCache; external storage binds the state machine to the in-memory Invoice struct.
patterns.example: sm := invoiceStateMachineCache.Get().(*InvoiceStateMachine); defer invoiceStateMachineCache.Put(sm)

patterns.name: advancementStrategy switches inline vs queued
patterns.description: advanceUntilStateStable publishes AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent under QueuedAdvancementStrategy, otherwise runs sm.AdvanceUntilStateStable inline.
patterns.example: if s.advancementStrategy == billing.QueuedAdvancementStrategy { return s.publisher.Publish(ctx, billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent{...}) }

patterns.name: Config+Validate New() pre-registers the line engine
patterns.description: New() builds billinglineengine.Engine internally and calls RegisterLineEngine so the invoice engine is always registered.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; svc := &Service{...}; invoiceEngine, _ := billinglineengine.New(...); svc.RegisterLineEngine(invoiceEngine); return svc, nil }

patterns.name: resolveTaxCodes ReadOnly flag
patterns.description: ReadOnly=true for simulate/preview (skips missing codes), ReadOnly=false only for real state-machine transitions (creates codes).
patterns.example: taxCodes, err := s.resolveTaxCodes(ctx, resolveTaxCodesInput{Namespace: ns, Invoice: out, ReadOnly: true})

key_file_guides.role: Service struct, Config validation, New constructor, transactionForInvoiceManipulation, WithAdvancementStrategy/WithLockedNamespaces options, RegisterStandardInvoiceHooks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All invoice-mutating helpers must call transactionForInvoiceManipulation; never start a raw tx without the customer-lock sequence.

key_file_guides.role: InvoiceStateMachine full state graph (draft → issuing → issued → payment states → paid/overdue/uncollectible/voided + delete/updating).
key_file_guides.watch_for: New states must be added to allocateStateMachine() Configure calls AND the StatusDetails resolver; the sync.Pool reuses machines — reset Invoice before use.

key_file_guides.role: InvoicePendingLines / prepareBillableLines — progressive billing line splitting, collection cutoff, per-currency invoice creation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolvePendingLineCollectionCutoff has three alignment modes (bypass, subscription, anchored); add new kinds here.
Avg/Min aggregations unsupported for split lines — validate in getFeatureUsage.Validate(); semaphore = maxParallelQuantitySnapshots.

key_file_guides.role: Pure stateless calculation pipeline sub-package; injected as invoicecalc.Calculator; mockable via WithInvoiceCalculator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All dependency data (FeatureMeters, TaxCodes, RatingService) must be resolved before Calculate — the pipeline performs no I/O.
Default-profile changes trigger customer re-pinning; all apps in a profile must share AppID (lo.Uniq check).

key_file_guides.role: resolveTaxCodes: gathers unique tax codes from lines + DefaultTaxConfig, looks up or creates them.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call with ReadOnly=false from simulate/preview paths — it creates tax-code rows.

key_file_guides.role: CreatePendingInvoiceLines: validates, resolves/upserts gathering invoice, normalizes/upserts lines, publishes GatheringInvoiceCreated.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Lines with ChargeID set must have Engine explicitly set (validated); engine routing via lineEngines.populateGatheringLineEngine.

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Adapter methods directly without transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue — bypasses the Ent ctx-bound transaction
Mutating customer invoices without transactionForInvoiceManipulation — omits the advisory lock and races
Calling resolveTaxCodes with ReadOnly=false from simulate/preview paths — creates tax-code rows unexpectedly
Adding a StandardInvoiceStatus without configuring it in allocateStateMachine() — the state machine panics on unknown state
Importing billinglineengine from outside billingservice for direct line computation — go through RegisterLineEngine and the LineEngine interface

decisions.decision: sync.Pool for InvoiceStateMachine to reduce GC pressure on high-frequency advancement
decisions.rationale: State machines are large structs with closure-bound state graphs; pooling avoids repeated allocation on the hot billing-worker path.

decisions.decision: Lock sequence: UpsertCustomerLock outside the tx, then LockCustomerForUpdate inside
decisions.rationale: The advisory lock row must exist before SELECT FOR UPDATE can acquire it; the two-step pattern prevents deadlocks under concurrent invoice creation.

decisions.decision: advancementStrategy allows queued vs inline state-machine advancement
decisions.rationale: Production uses queued advancement via billing-worker Kafka events to avoid long transactions; tests use inline for determinism.

code_examples.scenario: Adding an invoice mutation method that must hold the customer lock

openmeter/billing/validators.purpose: Organisational package grouping cross-domain billing guard validators (customer/, subscription/) that register pre/post-mutation hooks into customer.Service and subscription.Service, enforcing billing pre-conditions without creating import cycles between the billing, customer, and subscription packages.

patterns.name: Registration at wiring time, not via cross-domain import
patterns.description: Each validator is constructed in app/common and registered via customerService.RegisterRequestValidator() or subscriptionService.RegisterHook(); coupling lives in the DI layer only.
patterns.example: // app/common: v := NewValidator(...); customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(v)

patterns.name: Embed noop base structs
patterns.description: Validators embed customer.NoopRequestValidator or subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook so they override only the lifecycle methods they need.
patterns.example: type Validator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; billingService billing.Service }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Each file asserts compliance with var _ Interface = (*Validator)(nil).
patterns.example: var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil)

patterns.name: Nil-guard constructors returning error
patterns.description: NewValidator checks each injected service for nil, catching misconfigured wiring at startup.
patterns.example: if billingService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("billing service is required") }

patterns.name: models.NewGenericConflictError for billing setup failures
patterns.description: subscription/validator.go wraps failures so the HTTP layer renders 409.
patterns.example: return models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("invalid billing setup: %w", err))

patterns.name: Sync-before-validate in customer deletion
patterns.description: customer/customer.go calls SynchronizeSubscription before ListInvoices so pending charges are reflected before the gate check.
patterns.example: v.syncService.SynchronizeSubscription(ctx, view, time.Now()) // must precede ListInvoices

patterns.name: errors.Join for multi-entity validation
patterns.description: customer/customer.go collects per-invoice errors and returns errors.Join so the caller sees all blocking invoices at once.
patterns.example: return errors.Join(errs...)

key_file_guides.role: Implements customer.RequestValidator; ValidateDeleteCustomer syncs subscriptions then blocks deletion when non-final standard invoices or active gathering invoices exist.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Skipping SynchronizeSubscription before ListInvoices causes false-negative gate checks; subscriptionService is intentionally nullable — check app/common wiring before adding a nil guard.

key_file_guides.role: Implements subscription.SubscriptionCommandHook via AfterCreate/AfterUpdate; validates the customer's billing app supports tax, invoicing, and payment collection before a priced subscription activates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: hasBillableItems short-circuits free subscriptions — re-check for new ratecard types; capability slice is hardcoded to three app.CapabilityType values.

anti_patterns: Adding ValidateCreateCustomer/ValidateUpdateCustomer without a billing-domain reason — this package only guards delete
Calling billing.Adapter or Ent directly from either validator — all reads must go through billing.Service
Returning a plain error instead of models.NewGenericConflictError for billing setup failures in the subscription validator
Adding app-type-specific branching (e.g. 'if Stripe do X') — capability validation is intentionally app-agnostic
Using context.Background() instead of propagating the incoming ctx

decisions.decision: Validators live under billing/validators/ rather than inside customer/ or subscription/
decisions.rationale: Billing has dependency edges to both customer and subscription; placing validators here avoids import cycles by keeping billing-domain logic in billing's own tree.

decisions.decision: Subscription sync driven inside the customer deletion validator, not as a subscription-service pre-hook
decisions.rationale: Ensures invoice state is current at deletion time while keeping the customer package free of billing concerns.

decisions.decision: Post-create/post-update (AfterCreate/AfterUpdate) hooks for subscription validation rather than pre-create
decisions.rationale: Billing setup validation checks state that only exists after persistence (override profile, app assignment); a pre-create check would race incomplete state.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new billing validator (embed noop, nil-guard constructor, compile-time assertion)
